title: Parser-matcher integration: wire ExtractedCommand into eval pipeline + builder DSL + safe-pattern evaluation
description:
FOUNDATIONAL TASK — everything else depends on this.

Three tightly coupled changes that fix the core architecture gap identified across plans 02, 03a, 03b:

1. **Parser-matcher integration**: The eval pipeline (internal/eval/pipeline.go) currently passes raw command strings to Rule.Match(command string). It must instead call the tree-sitter parser to get an ExtractedCommand, then pass that to matchers. Change MatchFunc from func(command string) bool to func(cmd parse.ExtractedCommand) bool. Update all existing rules in internal/packs/ to use structured matching on ExtractedCommand fields (Name, Args, Flags) instead of strings.Contains.

2. **Matcher DSL / builder pattern**: Implement the planned matcher types: CommandMatcher interface, NameMatcher, FlagMatcher, ArgMatcher, CompositeMatcher. Implement builder functions: packs.Name(), packs.ArgAt(), packs.Flags(), packs.And(), packs.Or(), packs.Not(). These are specified in plan 02-matching-framework.md.

3. **Safe-pattern evaluation**: The pipeline currently only evaluates destructive rules. Implement safe rule evaluation with short-circuit semantics: if a command matches a safe pattern, skip destructive evaluation for that pack. This is a P0 gap in plans 03a and 03b.

Affects: internal/eval/pipeline.go, internal/packs/registry.go, internal/packs/defaults.go, internal/packs/database.go, new files for matcher types.
Tests: Update all existing tests, add matcher DSL unit tests, safe-pattern interaction tests.

comments.author: Danny Cosson
comments.text: Completed. Parser-matcher integration, builder DSL, safe-pattern short-circuiting, and core-pack registration regression fix (69fb99c) are in. Remaining non-blocking follow-ups tracked under dcg-c9c.9: allocation regression + env-escalation golden compatibility.
